EMG Fatigue Analysis Pipeline

Morgan Virolan

2026-02-20

1 Scientific question and Objectives

This is the companion notebook for the AMSTROM Campain, where we analyze the sEMG and torque data from the fatigue protocol. It’s main objectives is (at this point) to quntifify the fatigue-induced changes in sEMG features (RMS, MDF) during the dynamic (EX_DYN) and static (EX_STA) fatigue sequences, and to see how they relate to the torque loss. It’s the first step of the broader study which will also include the analysis of other physiological signals recorded during the same experimental protocole (BIA, NIRS, Mecanical values) + stats & ML.

Dataset structure Signal assumptions (delsys hardware band-pass ?? “slow” ramp up -> cut middle sequence + fist windows on STA)

Loads packages.

library(arrow)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(ggplot2)
library(signal)   # butter(), filtfilt()
library(glue)


options(dplyr.summarise.inform = FALSE)

11 1. LOAD + PREPARE DATA

11.0.1 Pathing & loading

RUN_ID <- "101SaCl_20251127"
subject_directory <- "results/DATA_EXPORT" |> 
  file.path(RUN_ID) |>
  glue::glue()

master_file_path <- file.path(subject_directory, "02_master.parquet")
emg_file_path    <- file.path(subject_directory, "02_emg_compact.parquet")
torque_file_path <- file.path(subject_directory, "02_torque_compact.parquet")

master_table <- read_parquet(master_file_path) 
emg_table    <- read_parquet(emg_file_path)    
torque_table <- read_parquet(torque_file_path)

11.0.2 Creating central DataFrame

analysis_table <- master_table |>
  # Join EMG, but remove the redundant columns first
  inner_join(
    emg_table |> select(-VC, -VC_count, -SEQ, -SEQ_index), 
    by = "time_index"
  ) |>
  # Join Torque, but remove the redundant columns first
  inner_join(
    torque_table |> select(-VC, -VC_count, -SEQ, -SEQ_index), 
    by = "time_index"
  )

cat("analysis_table:", nrow(analysis_table), "x", ncol(analysis_table), "\n")
## analysis_table: 1306102 x 10
print(names(analysis_table))
##  [1] "time_index" "time_ref_s" "SEQ_index"  "SEQ"        "VC"        
##  [6] "VC_count"   "emg6"       "emg8"       "emg10"      "torque_raw"

11.0.3 Grab the CONTROL sequence (MVC/SVC/MVC_RECOVERY)

# MVC_REF = 3 to 4 reps of maximal voluntary contraction (MVC) at the start of the session
mvc_ref_table <- analysis_table |>
  dplyr::filter(.data[["SEQ"]] == "MVC_REF",
              .data[["VC"]] == 1) |> # only keep actual reps (voluntary contraction = 1) / no rest
  dplyr::arrange(.data[["time_index"]])


#SVC_REF = submaximal voluntary contractions at 30/50/70% of MVC, used for calibration of torque and EMG
svc_ref_table <- analysis_table |>
  dplyr::filter(.data[["SEQ"]] == "SVC_REF",
              .data[["VC"]] == 1) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(.data[["time_index"]])


#MVC_RECOV_DYN = MVC checkup after the dynamic fatigue sequence (EX_DYN) + 5 mins rest -  used to assess recovery oafter fatigue

mvc_recov_dyn_table <- analysis_table |>
  dplyr::filter(.data[["SEQ"]] == "MVC_RECOV_DYN",
              .data[["VC"]] == 1) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(.data[["time_index"]])


#MMVC_RECOV_STA = MVC checkup after the static fatigue sequence (EX_STA) + 5 mins rest -  used to assess recovery after fatigue

mvc_recov_sta_table <- analysis_table |>
  dplyr::filter(.data[["SEQ"]] == "MVC_RECOV_STA",
              .data[["VC"]] == 1) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(.data[["time_index"]])


########

#stack the tables temporarily and plot them
bind_rows(mvc_ref_table, svc_ref_table, mvc_recov_dyn_table, mvc_recov_sta_table) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = time_ref_s, y = torque_raw, color = SEQ)) +
  geom_line() +
  # creates the series of subplots (facets)
  facet_wrap(~SEQ, scales = "free") + 
  theme_minimal()

### Grab the FATIGUE sequences

## EX_DYN = dynamic fatigue sequence (62 reps, 3s efforts 2s recup, max effort each rep)
ex_dyn_table <- analysis_table |>
  dplyr::filter(.data[["SEQ"]] == "EX_DYN",
                .data[["VC"]] == 1) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data[["time_index"]])

## EX_STA = static fatigue sequence (1 rep, 60s effort, 70% of baseline mvc)
ex_sta_table <- analysis_table |>
  dplyr::filter(.data[["SEQ"]] == "EX_STA",
                .data[["VC"]] == 1) |> #just in case sequencing is loose (everything should be VC==1)
  dplyr::arrange(.data[["time_index"]])


##plot
bind_rows(
  ex_dyn_table |> dplyr::mutate(SEQ = "EX_DYN"),
  ex_sta_table |> dplyr::mutate(SEQ = "EX_STA")
) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = time_ref_s, y = torque_raw, color = SEQ)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~SEQ, scales = "free") + 
  theme_minimal()

11.0.4 Torque calibration (Raw -> Nm)

#set values, from the biodex calibration
torque_offset_volt <- 0.106
torque_gain_Nm_per_volt <- 145.30

calc_torque_nm <- function(df) {
df |> dplyr::mutate(torque_Nm = 145.30 * (torque_raw - 0.106))
}

#convert raw torque (microvolt) to physical units (Nm) for all the tables
ex_dyn_table <- calc_torque_nm(ex_dyn_table)
ex_sta_table <- calc_torque_nm(ex_sta_table)
mvc_ref_table <- calc_torque_nm(mvc_ref_table)
svc_ref_table <- calc_torque_nm(svc_ref_table)
mvc_recov_dyn_table <- calc_torque_nm(mvc_recov_dyn_table)
mvc_recov_sta_table <- calc_torque_nm(mvc_recov_sta_table)

#print the 10 lines of each + 10 lines of the raw table to see the difference
print(head(ex_dyn_table |> dplyr::select(time_ref_s, torque_raw, torque_Nm), 10))

#remove the analysis table  - we extracted what we need for now
rm(analysis_table)
rm(emg_table)
rm(torque_table)
gc()

11.0.6 Compute NM references baseline (full rep, nm)

 #calculate : mean of ALL (3 or 4) MVC_refs reps
mvc_ref_value <- mean(mvc_ref_table$torque_Nm[mvc_ref_table$VC_count == 1])
message("mean mvc_ref value = ", mvc_ref_value)
## mean mvc_ref value = 212.002134048683
# same for svc_ref : filtered by vc_count number
# Filter by the VC_count number
svc_30_value <- mean(svc_ref_table$torque_Nm[svc_ref_table$VC_count == 1])
message("mean svc_30_value = ", svc_30_value)
## mean svc_30_value = 71.1234309551462
svc_50_value <- mean(svc_ref_table$torque_Nm[svc_ref_table$VC_count == 2])
message("mean svc_50_value = ", svc_50_value)
## mean svc_50_value = 103.963827843621
svc_70_value <- mean(svc_ref_table$torque_Nm[svc_ref_table$VC_count == 3])
message("mean svc_70_value = ", svc_70_value)
## mean svc_70_value = 152.951173020114
#calculate : mean of each MVC_recov_DYN / MVC_recov_STA reps
mvc_recov_dyn_value <- mean(mvc_recov_dyn_table$torque_Nm[mvc_recov_dyn_table$VC_count == 1])
message("mean mvc_recov_dyn value = ", mvc_recov_dyn_value)
## mean mvc_recov_dyn value = 207.572796342795
mvc_recov_sta_value <- mean(mvc_recov_sta_table$torque_Nm[mvc_recov_sta_table$VC_count == 1])
message("mean mvc_recov_sta value = ", mvc_recov_sta_value)
## mean mvc_recov_sta value = 215.69563521492
#verif

print(svc_30_value / mvc_ref_value) # should be around 0.3)
## [1] 0.3354845
print(svc_50_value / mvc_ref_value) # should be around 0.5)
## [1] 0.4903905
print(svc_70_value / mvc_ref_value) # should be around 0.7)
## [1] 0.7214605
print(mvc_recov_dyn_value / mvc_ref_value) # should be around 1 if full recovery, <1 if not fully recovered
## [1] 0.9791071
print(mvc_recov_sta_value / mvc_ref_value) # should be around 1 if full recovery, <1 if not fully recovered
## [1] 1.017422

12 2. FILTERING

12.0.1 sEMG Filtering func

emg_sampling_frequency_hz <- 2148.149

emg_bandpass_low_hz <- 20
emg_bandpass_high_hz <- 450
emg_filter_order <- 4

## Maybe band filter BEFORE rather than each individual chunks ?? Find out.

# BANDPASS FILTER -----------------------------------
# clean artefacts, movement related noises, high freq noise.
emg_bandpass_filter <- function(emg_signal, sampling_frequency_hz, 
                                low_hz, high_hz, filter_order) {
  
  # Centers signal around zero by subtracting the mean.
  # This prevents a large "spike" at 0Hz in the frequency domain (dc offset removal)
  emg_centered <- emg_signal - mean(emg_signal)
  
  # Nyquist Normalization : cutoffs needed for digital filters
  # ratio of the Nyquist frequency (half the sampling rate).
  normalized_cutoffs <- c(low_hz, high_hz) / (sampling_frequency_hz / 2)
  
  # Butterworth filter 
  butter_filter <- signal::butter(filter_order, normalized_cutoffs, type = "pass")
  
  # Zero-Phase Filtering
  # filter twice (forward and backward) -> no time-shift (phase lag
  signal::filtfilt(butter_filter, emg_centered)
}

# RMS (Root Mean Square) Function -----------------------------------
# Squares every point (-> everything becomes positive), finds average, takes the root -> power of the signal (same unit). amplitude/intensity of the contraction.
emg_rms <- function(emg_signal) {
  sqrt(mean(emg_signal^2))
}

# Median Frequency  -----------------------------------
# Frequency at which the power spectrum is divided into two equal parts (50% below/ 50% above hz). As fatigue sets in, the median frequency tends to decrease (shift towards lower frequencies).

emg_median_frequency_hz <- function(emg_signal, sampling_frequency_hz) {
  emg_centered <- emg_signal - mean(emg_signal) #recompute, last one is nested and global var is a dangerous world.
  n_samples <- length(emg_centered)
# Hann window to reduce spectral leakage (needed for us ?)
  hann_window <- 0.5 - 0.5 * cos(2 * pi * (0:(n_samples - 1)) / (n_samples - 1))
  emg_windowed <- emg_centered * hann_window

# Single-sided power spectrum (FFT)
  fft_complex <- stats::fft(emg_windowed)
  n_half <- floor(n_samples / 2) + 1
  fft_half <- fft_complex[1:n_half]
  
# Power spectrum (magnitude squared of the FFT, normalized by the number of samples)
  power_spectrum <- (Mod(fft_half) ^ 2) / n_samples
  frequency_axis_hz <- (0:(n_half - 1)) * (sampling_frequency_hz / n_samples)
  
  #drop 0hz bin
  power_spectrum <- power_spectrum[-1]
frequency_axis_hz <- frequency_axis_hz[-1]

  # Cumulative power to find the median frequency
  cumulative_power <- cumsum(power_spectrum)
  median_index <- which(cumulative_power >= 0.5 * sum(power_spectrum))[1]
  
  frequency_axis_hz[median_index]
  }

13 3. FEATURES COMPUTATION

13.0.1 Apply Filtering, Slicing windows & Features computation

compute_rep_emg_features <- function(rep_table, emg_column_name) {

  rep_split <- split(rep_table, rep_table$VC_count)

  feature_table <- do.call(rbind,
    lapply(names(rep_split), function(vc_id) {

      rep_df <- rep_split[[vc_id]]

      # Filter FULL REP (so even if clipping artefact, it doesnt matter downstream)
      emg_filt <- emg_bandpass_filter(
        emg_signal = rep_df[[emg_column_name]],
        sampling_frequency_hz = emg_sampling_frequency_hz,
        low_hz = emg_bandpass_low_hz,
        high_hz = emg_bandpass_high_hz,
        filter_order = emg_filter_order
      )

      # grab exactly the middle seconds (so we avoid artefact, plateau, easier to compare between different duration)
      t_start <- min(rep_df$time_ref_s)
      t_end   <- max(rep_df$time_ref_s)
      t_mid   <- (t_start + t_end) / 2

      mid_start <- t_mid - 0.5
      mid_end   <- t_mid + 0.5

      mid_idx <- which(rep_df$time_ref_s >= mid_start &
                       rep_df$time_ref_s <  mid_end)

      emg_mid <- emg_filt[mid_idx]

      # compute features on the middle 1s window
      data.frame(
        VC_count = as.integer(vc_id),
        #compute rms on the extracted middle sec
        rms_mid_1s = emg_rms(emg_mid),
        # compute mdf on the extracted middle sec
        mdf_mid_1s_hz =
          emg_median_frequency_hz(emg_mid,
                                  sampling_frequency_hz = emg_sampling_frequency_hz)
      )
    })
  )

  feature_table |>
    dplyr::arrange(VC_count)
}

13.0.1.1 QC plotting (filtering, slicing, feature comp)

reps_to_plot <- c(3)   # 1-62 for EX_DYN, 1 for EX_STA, 1-3 or 4 for MVC_REF 
emg_col <- "emg6"            # 6, 8 or 10

for (rep_id in reps_to_plot) {

  rep_df <- mvc_ref_table |> dplyr::filter(VC_count == rep_id)

  # Filter full rep 
  emg_raw <- rep_df[[emg_col]]
  emg_filt <- emg_bandpass_filter(
    emg_signal = emg_raw,
    sampling_frequency_hz = emg_sampling_frequency_hz,
    low_hz = emg_bandpass_low_hz,
    high_hz = emg_bandpass_high_hz,
    filter_order = emg_filter_order
  )

  # grab middle 1s
  t_start <- min(rep_df$time_ref_s)
  t_end   <- max(rep_df$time_ref_s)
  t_mid   <- (t_start + t_end) / 2

  mid_start <- t_mid - 0.5
  mid_end   <- t_mid + 0.5

  mid_mask <- rep_df$time_ref_s >= mid_start & rep_df$time_ref_s < mid_end
  emg_mid <- emg_filt[mid_mask]

  # Compute features on middle 1s
  rms_val <- emg_rms(emg_mid)
  mdf_val <- emg_median_frequency_hz(emg_mid, sampling_frequency_hz = emg_sampling_frequency_hz)

  # Spectrum of middle 1s 
  n <- length(emg_mid)
  hann <- 0.5 - 0.5 * cos(2*pi*(0:(n-1))/(n-1))
  xw <- (emg_mid - mean(emg_mid)) * hann

  X <- stats::fft(xw)
  n_half <- floor(n/2) + 1
  P <- (Mod(X[1:n_half])^2) / n
  f <- (0:(n_half-1)) * (emg_sampling_frequency_hz / n)

  # Plot layout (3 panels)
  old_par <- par(no.readonly = TRUE)
  par(mfrow = c(3, 1), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1))

  # 1) Raw EMG
  plot(rep_df$time_ref_s, emg_raw, type = "l",
       main = paste0("Rep ", rep_id, " — RAW ", emg_col),
       xlab = "Time (s)", ylab = "EMG")

  # 2) Filtered EMG + highlight middle 1s
  plot(rep_df$time_ref_s, emg_filt, type = "l",
       main = paste0("Rep ", rep_id, " - Filtered (20–450 Hz) + middle 1s | RMS=", signif(rms_val, 4),
                     " MDF=", signif(mdf_val, 4), " Hz"),
       xlab = "Time (s)", ylab = "EMG")
  lines(rep_df$time_ref_s[mid_mask], emg_filt[mid_mask], col = "red", lwd = 2)

  # 3) Power spectrum of middle 1s + MDF
  plot(f, P, type = "l",
       main = paste0("Rep ", rep_id, " - Power spectrum (middle 1s)"),
       xlab = "Frequency (Hz)", ylab = "Power")
  abline(v = mdf_val, col = "red", lwd = 2)

  par(old_par)
}

13.0.2 Compute FEATURES reference table

build_ref_features_one <- function(seq_table, seq_name, channel_name) {
  feat <- compute_rep_emg_features(seq_table, channel_name)
  feat$SEQ <- seq_name
  feat$channel <- channel_name
  feat <- feat |> dplyr::select(SEQ, channel, VC_count, rms_mid_1s, mdf_mid_1s_hz)
  feat
}

## build the feature table
reference_rep_feature_table <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  # MVC baseline (3–4 reps)
  build_ref_features_one(mvc_ref_table, "MVC_REF", "emg6"),
  build_ref_features_one(mvc_ref_table, "MVC_REF", "emg8"),
  build_ref_features_one(mvc_ref_table, "MVC_REF", "emg10"),

  # SVC reference (1 rep per intensity ((30/50/70%)) stored in VC_count 1/2/3)
  build_ref_features_one(svc_ref_table, "SVC_REF", "emg6"),
  build_ref_features_one(svc_ref_table, "SVC_REF", "emg8"),
  build_ref_features_one(svc_ref_table, "SVC_REF", "emg10"),

  # Recovery checks (usually 1 rep)
  build_ref_features_one(mvc_recov_dyn_table, "MVC_RECOV_DYN", "emg6"),
  build_ref_features_one(mvc_recov_dyn_table, "MVC_RECOV_DYN", "emg8"),
  build_ref_features_one(mvc_recov_dyn_table, "MVC_RECOV_DYN", "emg10"),

  build_ref_features_one(mvc_recov_sta_table, "MVC_RECOV_STA", "emg6"),
  build_ref_features_one(mvc_recov_sta_table, "MVC_RECOV_STA", "emg8"),
  build_ref_features_one(mvc_recov_sta_table, "MVC_RECOV_STA", "emg10")
) |>
  dplyr::arrange(SEQ, channel, VC_count)

print(reference_rep_feature_table)
##              SEQ channel VC_count   rms_mid_1s mdf_mid_1s_hz

## 15       MVC_REF    emg8        3 6.250647e-05      248.3409

## 23       SVC_REF    emg8        2 3.410049e-05      248.3409
## 24       SVC_REF    emg8        3 4.619650e-05      244.6733
## create a summary table with mean and sd for each sequence, channel and intensity (for svc_ref)
reference_summary_table <- reference_rep_feature_table |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    intensity_label = dplyr::case_when(
      SEQ == "SVC_REF" & VC_count == 1 ~ "SVC_30",
      SEQ == "SVC_REF" & VC_count == 2 ~ "SVC_50",
      SEQ == "SVC_REF" & VC_count == 3 ~ "SVC_70",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::group_by(SEQ, channel, intensity_label) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_reps = dplyr::n(),
    rms_mean = mean(rms_mid_1s),
    rms_sd   = ifelse(dplyr::n() > 1, sd(rms_mid_1s), NA_real_),
    mdf_mean_hz = mean(mdf_mid_1s_hz),
    mdf_sd_hz   = ifelse(dplyr::n() > 1, sd(mdf_mid_1s_hz), NA_real_),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(SEQ, channel, intensity_label)

print(reference_summary_table)
## # A tibble: 18 × 8
##    SEQ    channel intensity_label n_reps rms_mean   rms_sd mdf_mean_hz mdf_sd_hz

##  1 MVC_R… emg10   <NA>                 1  1.26e-4 NA              248.     NA   
##  2 MVC_R… emg6    <NA>                 1  1.20e-4 NA              265.     NA   
##  3 MVC_R… emg8    <NA>                 1  7.07e-5 NA              257.     NA   
##  4 MVC_R… emg10   <NA>                 1  1.40e-4 NA              257.     NA   
##  5 MVC_R… emg6    <NA>                 1  1.34e-4 NA              273.     NA   
##  6 MVC_R… emg8    <NA>                 1  7.80e-5 NA              261.     NA

##  8 MVC_R… emg6    <NA>                 3  8.81e-5  3.21e-6        261.      4.14

## 10 SVC_R… emg10   SVC_30               1  2.34e-5 NA              236.     NA

13.0.2.1 Plot QC (feature ref comp)

reference_summary_table <- reference_rep_feature_table |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    intensity_label = dplyr::case_when(
      SEQ == "SVC_REF" & VC_count == 1 ~ "SVC_30",
      SEQ == "SVC_REF" & VC_count == 2 ~ "SVC_50",
      SEQ == "SVC_REF" & VC_count == 3 ~ "SVC_70",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::group_by(SEQ, channel, intensity_label) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_reps = dplyr::n(),
    rms_mean = mean(rms_mid_1s),
    rms_sd   = ifelse(dplyr::n() > 1, sd(rms_mid_1s), NA_real_),
    mdf_mean_hz = mean(mdf_mid_1s_hz),
    mdf_sd_hz   = ifelse(dplyr::n() > 1, sd(mdf_mid_1s_hz), NA_real_),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(SEQ, channel, intensity_label)

print(reference_summary_table)
## # A tibble: 18 × 8

##  8 MVC_R… emg6    <NA>                 3  8.81e-5  3.21e-6        261.      4.14

## 10 SVC_R… emg10   SVC_30               1  2.34e-5 NA              236.     NA   
## 11 SVC_R… emg10   SVC_50               1  4.10e-5 NA              286.     NA   
## 12 SVC_R… emg10   SVC_70               1  6.18e-5 NA              257.     NA

## 14 SVC_R… emg6    SVC_50               1  5.61e-5 NA              290.     NA

###

reference_rep_feature_table |>
  dplyr::filter(SEQ == "MVC_REF") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(VC_count),
             y = rms_mid_1s,
             color = channel,
             group = channel)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    title = "MVC_REF RMS (middle 1 s) per rep",
    x = "MVC repetition",
    y = "RMS"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

##

reference_rep_feature_table |>
  dplyr::filter(SEQ %in% c("MVC_REF", "MVC_RECOV_DYN", "MVC_RECOV_STA")) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = SEQ,
             y = rms_mid_1s,
             color = channel)) +
  geom_point(position = position_jitter(width = 0.05), size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "MVC baseline vs recovery RMS",
    y = "RMS"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

13.0.3 Compute MVC/EMG Baseline

mvc_baseline_table <- reference_rep_feature_table |>
  dplyr::filter(SEQ == "MVC_REF") |>
  dplyr::group_by(channel) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    rms_mvc_baseline = mean(rms_mid_1s),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

mvc_baseline_table
## # A tibble: 3 × 2
##   channel rms_mvc_baseline
##   <chr>              <dbl>
## 1 emg10          0.0000938
## 2 emg6           0.0000881
## 3 emg8           0.0000679

14 3.1 Dynamic fatigue analysis

14.1 Compute & normalize EX_DYN rep features

ex_dyn_feature_table <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  compute_rep_emg_features(ex_dyn_table, "emg6")  |> dplyr::mutate(channel = "emg6"),
  compute_rep_emg_features(ex_dyn_table, "emg8")  |> dplyr::mutate(channel = "emg8"),
  compute_rep_emg_features(ex_dyn_table, "emg10") |> dplyr::mutate(channel = "emg10")
) |>
  dplyr::arrange(channel, VC_count)

### normalize
ex_dyn_feature_table <- ex_dyn_feature_table |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    mvc_baseline_table |>
      dplyr::select(channel, rms_mvc_baseline),
    by = "channel"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(rms_norm = rms_mid_1s / rms_mvc_baseline)

# check the range of the normalized rms values per channel
ex_dyn_feature_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(channel) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    min_rms_norm = min(rms_norm),
    max_rms_norm = max(rms_norm),
    mean_rms_norm = mean(rms_norm),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
## # A tibble: 3 × 4
##   channel min_rms_norm max_rms_norm mean_rms_norm
##   <chr>          <dbl>        <dbl>         <dbl>
## 1 emg10          0.537         1.34         0.982
## 2 emg6           0.506         1.45         0.940
## 3 emg8           0.618         1.35         1.03
#plot

ggplot(ex_dyn_feature_table, aes(VC_count, rms_norm, color = channel)) +
  geom_line() +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(ex_dyn_feature_table, aes(VC_count, mdf_mid_1s_hz, color = channel)) +
  geom_line() +
  theme_minimal()

#compute correlation table

ex_dyn_correlation_table <- ex_dyn_feature_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(channel) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    r_rms = cor(rms_norm, VC_count),
    r_mdf = cor(mdf_mid_1s_hz, VC_count),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ex_dyn_correlation_table
## # A tibble: 3 × 3
##   channel  r_rms  r_mdf
##   <chr>    <dbl>  <dbl>
## 1 emg10   -0.485 -0.649
## 2 emg6    -0.635 -0.724
## 3 emg8    -0.360 -0.305
ggplot(ex_dyn_feature_table, aes(x = VC_count, y = rms_norm)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ channel, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = "Rep (VC_count)",
    y = "RMS normalized (MVC_REF baseline)"
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

ggplot(ex_dyn_feature_table, aes(x = VC_count, y = mdf_mid_1s_hz)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ channel, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = "Rep (VC_count)",
    y = "Median Frequency (Hz)"
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

#compute magnitude

ex_dyn_trend_table <- ex_dyn_feature_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(channel) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_reps = dplyr::n(),

    rms_slope_per_rep =
      coef(lm(rms_norm ~ VC_count))[["VC_count"]],
    rms_r =
      cor(rms_norm, VC_count),

    mdf_slope_hz_per_rep =
      coef(lm(mdf_mid_1s_hz ~ VC_count))[["VC_count"]],
    mdf_r =
      cor(mdf_mid_1s_hz, VC_count),

    .groups = "drop"
  )

ex_dyn_trend_table
## # A tibble: 3 × 6
##   channel n_reps rms_slope_per_rep  rms_r mdf_slope_hz_per_rep  mdf_r
##   <chr>    <int>             <dbl>  <dbl>                <dbl>  <dbl>
## 1 emg10       62          -0.00511 -0.485               -0.991 -0.649
## 2 emg6        62          -0.00710 -0.635               -1.37  -0.724
## 3 emg8        62          -0.00319 -0.360               -0.299 -0.305

#total drop

ex_dyn_drop_table <- ex_dyn_feature_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(channel) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    rms_first = rms_norm[VC_count == min(VC_count)],
    rms_last  = rms_norm[VC_count == max(VC_count)],
    rms_percent_drop = (rms_last - rms_first) / rms_first * 100,

    mdf_first = mdf_mid_1s_hz[VC_count == min(VC_count)],
    mdf_last  = mdf_mid_1s_hz[VC_count == max(VC_count)],
    mdf_percent_drop = (mdf_last - mdf_first) / mdf_first * 100,

    .groups = "drop"
  )

ex_dyn_drop_table
## # A tibble: 3 × 7
##   channel rms_first rms_last rms_percent_drop mdf_first mdf_last
##   <chr>       <dbl>    <dbl>            <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1 emg10        1.15    1.12             -2.78      252.     220.
## 2 emg6         1.01    0.936            -7.72      257.     211.
## 3 emg8         1.07    1.24             15.9       252.     199.
## # ℹ 1 more variable: mdf_percent_drop <dbl>

#build ex_dyn torque per rep (middle 1s only)

ex_dyn_torque_table <- ex_dyn_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(VC_count) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    t_mid = mean(time_ref_s),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# now compute torque mid-window mean
ex_dyn_torque_feature_table <- ex_dyn_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(VC_count) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    t_mid = mean(time_ref_s)
  ) |>
  dplyr::filter(time_ref_s >= t_mid - 0.5,
                time_ref_s <= t_mid + 0.5) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    torque_mid_1s_Nm = mean(torque_Nm),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ex_dyn_torque_feature_table
## # A tibble: 62 × 2
##    VC_count torque_mid_1s_Nm
##       <int>            <dbl>
##  1        1             237.
##  2        2             254.
##  3        3             237.
##  4        4             237.
##  5        5             233.
##  6        6             230.
##  7        7             219.
##  8        8             208.
##  9        9             209.
## 10       10             209.
## # ℹ 52 more rows
# merge with emg feature table
ex_dyn_feature_table <- ex_dyn_feature_table |>
  dplyr::left_join(ex_dyn_torque_feature_table, by = "VC_count")
any(is.na(ex_dyn_feature_table$torque_mid_1s_Nm))
## [1] FALSE

#correlation feat vs torque

ex_dyn_torque_correlation <- ex_dyn_feature_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(channel) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    r_rms_vs_torque = cor(rms_norm, torque_mid_1s_Nm),
    r_mdf_vs_torque = cor(mdf_mid_1s_hz, torque_mid_1s_Nm),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ex_dyn_torque_correlation
## # A tibble: 3 × 3
##   channel r_rms_vs_torque r_mdf_vs_torque
##   <chr>             <dbl>           <dbl>
## 1 emg10             0.613           0.772
## 2 emg6              0.744           0.826
## 3 emg8              0.551           0.245

#torque fatigue magnitude

ex_dyn_torque_feature_table |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    torque_first = torque_mid_1s_Nm[VC_count == min(VC_count)],
    torque_last  = torque_mid_1s_Nm[VC_count == max(VC_count)],
    torque_percent_drop =
      (torque_last - torque_first) / torque_first * 100
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   torque_first torque_last torque_percent_drop
##          <dbl>       <dbl>               <dbl>
## 1         237.        126.               -47.0
range(ex_sta_table$time_ref_s)
## [1] 2066.670 2127.287
nrow(ex_sta_table)
## [1] 31432
names(ex_sta_table)
##  [1] "time_index" "time_ref_s" "SEQ_index"  "SEQ"        "VC"        
##  [6] "VC_count"   "emg6"       "emg8"       "emg10"      "torque_raw"
## [11] "torque_Nm"

15 3.2 Static Fatigue Analysis

#create the 1 s table

# EX_STA window grid (1 s, non-overlap)

window_length_s <- 1.0
window_step_s   <- 1.0

t_start <- min(ex_sta_table$time_ref_s)
t_end   <- max(ex_sta_table$time_ref_s)

window_start_s <- seq(t_start, t_end - window_length_s, by = window_step_s)

ex_sta_window_table <- tibble(
  window_id      = seq_along(window_start_s),
  window_start_s = window_start_s,
  window_end_s   = window_start_s + window_length_s,
  window_mid_s   = window_start_s + window_length_s / 2
)

# Quick sanity
nrow(ex_sta_window_table)
## [1] 60
ex_sta_window_table |> slice(1:5)
## # A tibble: 5 × 4
##   window_id window_start_s window_end_s window_mid_s
##       <int>          <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl>
## 1         1          2067.        2068.        2067.
## 2         2          2068.        2069.        2068.
## 3         3          2069.        2070.        2069.
## 4         4          2070.        2071.        2070.
## 5         5          2071.        2072.        2071.
# Torque mean per window (same windows)
ex_sta_torque_feature_table <- ex_sta_window_table |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(
    torque_mid_1s_Nm = mean(
      ex_sta_table$torque_Nm[
        ex_sta_table$time_ref_s >= window_start_s &
        ex_sta_table$time_ref_s <= window_end_s
      ],
      na.rm = TRUE
    )
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  select(window_id, window_mid_s, torque_mid_1s_Nm)

# Sanity
nrow(ex_sta_torque_feature_table)
## [1] 60
summary(ex_sta_torque_feature_table$torque_mid_1s_Nm)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   89.02  141.72  156.69  150.34  162.49  171.22

#compute emg features per window

library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

compute_ex_sta_window_emg_features <- function(sta_table, emg_column_name, window_table) {

  time_s  <- sta_table$time_ref_s
  emg_raw <- sta_table[[emg_column_name]]

  # Filter full EX_STA once (not the chunks)
  emg_filtered <- emg_bandpass_filter(
    emg_signal = emg_raw,
    sampling_frequency_hz = emg_sampling_frequency_hz,
    low_hz = emg_bandpass_low_hz,
    high_hz = emg_bandpass_high_hz,
    filter_order = emg_filter_order
  )

  out <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(window_table)), function(i) {

    w_start <- window_table$window_start_s[i]
    w_end   <- window_table$window_end_s[i]

    idx <- which(time_s >= w_start & time_s <= w_end)

    x <- emg_filtered[idx]

    tibble(
      window_id = window_table$window_id[i],
      window_mid_s = window_table$window_mid_s[i],
      rms_mid_1s = emg_rms(x),
      mdf_mid_1s_hz = emg_median_frequency_hz(x, emg_sampling_frequency_hz)
    )
  })

  bind_rows(out)
}

ex_sta_emg_feature_table <- bind_rows(
  compute_ex_sta_window_emg_features(ex_sta_table, "emg6",  ex_sta_window_table)  |> mutate(channel = "emg6"),
  compute_ex_sta_window_emg_features(ex_sta_table, "emg8",  ex_sta_window_table)  |> mutate(channel = "emg8"),
  compute_ex_sta_window_emg_features(ex_sta_table, "emg10", ex_sta_window_table) |> mutate(channel = "emg10")
) |>
  arrange(channel, window_id)

# Sanity
nrow(ex_sta_emg_feature_table)
## [1] 180
ex_sta_emg_feature_table |> slice(1:6)
## # A tibble: 6 × 5
##   window_id window_mid_s rms_mid_1s mdf_mid_1s_hz channel
##       <int>        <dbl>      <dbl>         <dbl> <chr>  
## 1         1        2067.  0.0000621          228. emg10  
## 2         2        2068.  0.000102           244. emg10  
## 3         3        2069.  0.000104           236. emg10  
## 4         4        2070.  0.000115           220. emg10  
## 5         5        2071.  0.000138           240. emg10  
## 6         6        2072.  0.000130           244. emg10
summary(ex_sta_emg_feature_table$rms_mid_1s)
##      Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. 
## 3.997e-05 7.560e-05 1.066e-04 1.035e-04 1.270e-04 1.595e-04
summary(ex_sta_emg_feature_table$mdf_mid_1s_hz)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   157.6   219.4   232.2   231.2   244.2   298.6
library(dplyr)

ex_sta_feature_table <- ex_sta_emg_feature_table |>
  # make rerunnable
  dplyr::select(-dplyr::any_of(c("rms_mvc_baseline", "rms_norm", "torque_mid_1s_Nm"))) |>
  dplyr::left_join(mvc_baseline_table, by = "channel") |>
  dplyr::mutate(rms_norm = rms_mid_1s / rms_mvc_baseline) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    ex_sta_torque_feature_table |> dplyr::select(window_id, torque_mid_1s_Nm),
    by = "window_id"
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(channel, window_id)

# Sanity
ex_sta_feature_table |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    any_na_baseline = any(is.na(rms_mvc_baseline)),
    any_na_norm = any(is.na(rms_norm)),
    any_na_torque = any(is.na(torque_mid_1s_Nm))
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   any_na_baseline any_na_norm any_na_torque
##   <lgl>           <lgl>       <lgl>        
## 1 FALSE           FALSE       FALSE

16 torque vs time

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(ex_sta_feature_table, aes(x = window_mid_s, y = torque_mid_1s_Nm)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    x = "Time (s)",
    y = "Torque (Nm)"
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

17 RMS_norm vs time

ggplot(ex_sta_feature_table, aes(x = window_mid_s, y = rms_norm)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ channel, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = "Time (s)",
    y = "RMS normalized (MVC_REF baseline)"
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

#mdf vs time

ggplot(ex_sta_feature_table, aes(x = window_mid_s, y = mdf_mid_1s_hz)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ channel, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = "Time (s)",
    y = "MDF (Hz)"
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

18 slopes & correlations vs time (ex_sta)

ex_sta_time_trend_table <- ex_sta_feature_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(channel) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_windows = dplyr::n(),

    torque_slope_per_s =
      coef(lm(torque_mid_1s_Nm ~ window_mid_s))[["window_mid_s"]],
    torque_r =
      cor(torque_mid_1s_Nm, window_mid_s),

    rms_slope_per_s =
      coef(lm(rms_norm ~ window_mid_s))[["window_mid_s"]],
    rms_r =
      cor(rms_norm, window_mid_s),

    mdf_slope_hz_per_s =
      coef(lm(mdf_mid_1s_hz ~ window_mid_s))[["window_mid_s"]],
    mdf_r =
      cor(mdf_mid_1s_hz, window_mid_s),

    .groups = "drop"
  )

ex_sta_time_trend_table
## # A tibble: 3 × 8
##   channel n_windows torque_slope_per_s torque_r rms_slope_per_s   rms_r
##   <chr>       <int>              <dbl>    <dbl>           <dbl>   <dbl>
## 1 emg10          60             -0.484   -0.483        0.000700  0.0597
## 2 emg6           60             -0.484   -0.483       -0.00530  -0.466 
## 3 emg8           60             -0.484   -0.483        0.00460   0.625 
## # ℹ 2 more variables: mdf_slope_hz_per_s <dbl>, mdf_r <dbl>

19 EX_STA EMG vs torque corr

ex_sta_torque_correlation <- ex_sta_feature_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(channel) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    r_rms_vs_torque = cor(rms_norm, torque_mid_1s_Nm),
    r_mdf_vs_torque = cor(mdf_mid_1s_hz, torque_mid_1s_Nm),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ex_sta_torque_correlation
## # A tibble: 3 × 3
##   channel r_rms_vs_torque r_mdf_vs_torque
##   <chr>             <dbl>           <dbl>
## 1 emg10            0.474            0.612
## 2 emg6             0.727            0.254
## 3 emg8             0.0599           0.186

#first vs last torque block

ex_sta_torque_feature_table |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    torque_first = torque_mid_1s_Nm[window_id == min(window_id)],
    torque_last  = torque_mid_1s_Nm[window_id == max(window_id)],
    torque_drop_Nm = torque_last - torque_first,
    torque_percent_drop =
      (torque_last - torque_first) / torque_first * 100
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 4
##   torque_first torque_last torque_drop_Nm torque_percent_drop
##          <dbl>       <dbl>          <dbl>               <dbl>
## 1         89.0        102.           12.9                14.5

#diag for identifying how many windows until plateau

ex_sta_torque_feature_table |>
  dplyr::mutate(rank_desc = rank(-torque_mid_1s_Nm)) |>
  dplyr::arrange(window_id) |>
  dplyr::select(window_id, window_mid_s, torque_mid_1s_Nm) |>
  head(10)
## # A tibble: 10 × 3
##    window_id window_mid_s torque_mid_1s_Nm
##        <int>        <dbl>            <dbl>
##  1         1        2067.             89.0
##  2         2        2068.            142. 
##  3         3        2069.            151. 
##  4         4        2070.            161. 
##  5         5        2071.            164. 
##  6         6        2072.            161. 
##  7         7        2073.            156. 
##  8         8        2074.            154. 
##  9         9        2075.            157. 
## 10        10        2076.            160.
ex_sta_torque_feature_table |>
  dplyr::arrange(desc(torque_mid_1s_Nm)) |>
  head(5)
## # A tibble: 5 × 3
##   window_id window_mid_s torque_mid_1s_Nm
##       <int>        <dbl>            <dbl>
## 1        29        2095.             171.
## 2        28        2094.             169.
## 3        27        2093.             168.
## 4        35        2101.             167.
## 5        18        2084.             167.

20 refining the MVC_REF baseline using the middle 1 s of each rep (instead of the global mean) and identifying the plateau onset in EX_STA (first window >= 70% of MVC_REF mid1s) to crop the analysis on the plateau+fatigue part.

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)


# MVC_REF torque baseline (middle 1 s per rep)

mvc_torque_mid1s_table <- mvc_ref_table |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$VC_count) |>
  dplyr::mutate(t_mid = mean(.data$time_ref_s)) |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$time_ref_s >= .data$t_mid - 0.5,
                .data$time_ref_s <= .data$t_mid + 0.5) |>
  dplyr::summarise(torque_mid_1s_Nm = mean(.data$torque_Nm), .groups = "drop")

mvc_ref_value_mid1s <- mean(mvc_torque_mid1s_table$torque_mid_1s_Nm)

mvc_ref_value_mid1s
## [1] 230.0556
# 70% target of Mid1s 

target_70 <- 0.70 * mvc_ref_value_mid1s

# start analysis from the first plateau onset (first window >= target)

plateau_window_id <- ex_sta_torque_feature_table |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$torque_mid_1s_Nm >= target_70) |>
  dplyr::slice(1) |>
  dplyr::pull(.data$window_id)

plateau_time_s <- ex_sta_torque_feature_table |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$window_id == plateau_window_id) |>
  dplyr::pull(.data$window_mid_s)

cat("MVC_REF mid1s mean torque =", round(mvc_ref_value_mid1s, 2), "Nm\n")
## MVC_REF mid1s mean torque = 230.06 Nm
cat("Target 70%               =", round(target_70, 2), "Nm\n")
## Target 70%               = 161.04 Nm
cat("Plateau starts at window =", plateau_window_id, "(t =", round(plateau_time_s, 2), "s)\n")
## Plateau starts at window = 4 (t = 2070.17 s)
#  QC plot torque windows

  ggplot(ex_sta_torque_feature_table, aes(x = window_mid_s, y = torque_mid_1s_Nm)) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_hline(yintercept = target_70, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = plateau_time_s, linetype = "dotted") +
    labs(x = "Time (s)", y = "Torque (Nm)",
         title = "EX_STA torque (1 s windows) — 70% target + plateau onset")

# Crop EX_STA to plateau+fatigue (drop ramp-up)

ex_sta_plateau_table <- ex_sta_feature_table |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$window_id >= plateau_window_id)

cat("Unique windows kept =", length(unique(ex_sta_plateau_table$window_id)), "\n")
## Unique windows kept = 57
# Plateau-only trends vs time

ex_sta_plateau_time_trend <- ex_sta_plateau_table |>
  group_by(channel) |>
  summarise(
    n_windows = n(),

    torque_slope_per_s = coef(lm(torque_mid_1s_Nm ~ window_mid_s))[["window_mid_s"]],
    torque_r = cor(torque_mid_1s_Nm, window_mid_s),

    rms_slope_per_s = coef(lm(rms_norm ~ window_mid_s))[["window_mid_s"]],
    rms_r = cor(rms_norm, window_mid_s),

    mdf_slope_hz_per_s = coef(lm(mdf_mid_1s_hz ~ window_mid_s))[["window_mid_s"]],
    mdf_r = cor(mdf_mid_1s_hz, window_mid_s),

    .groups = "drop"
  )

ex_sta_plateau_time_trend
## # A tibble: 3 × 8
##   channel n_windows torque_slope_per_s torque_r rms_slope_per_s  rms_r
##   <chr>       <int>              <dbl>    <dbl>           <dbl>  <dbl>
## 1 emg10          57             -0.702   -0.733        -0.00154 -0.141
## 2 emg6           57             -0.702   -0.733        -0.00717 -0.602
## 3 emg8           57             -0.702   -0.733         0.00397  0.582
## # ℹ 2 more variables: mdf_slope_hz_per_s <dbl>, mdf_r <dbl>
sum(ex_sta_torque_feature_table$torque_mid_1s_Nm >= target_70, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 22

```

sessionInfo()
